name: reply
title: Discord Reply
description: Reply on the messaging channel. Pass chat_id from the inbound message. Optionally pass reply_to, files, embeds, and components (buttons, selects, etc).

chat_id.description: Channel ID where the message will be sent

reply_to.description: Message ID to thread under. Use message_id from the inbound <channel> block, or an id from fetch.

files.description: Absolute file paths to attach (images, logs, etc). Max 10 files, 25MB each.

embeds.description: Discord embed objects. Fields: title, description, color (int), fields [{name, value, inline}], footer {text}, timestamp.

components.description: Discord message components. Use Action Rows containing Buttons, Select Menus, etc. See Discord Components V2 docs.

name: react
title: Reaction
description: Add an emoji reaction to a message. Unicode emoji work directly; custom emoji need the <:name:id> form.

message_id.description: ID of the message to react to

emoji.description: Unicode emoji (e.g. "👍") or custom emoji in <:name:id> format

name: edit_message
title: Edit Message
description: Edit a message the bot previously sent. Supports text, embeds, and components.

message_id.description: ID of the bot message to edit

name: download_attachment
title: Download Attachment
description: Download attachments from a message to the local inbox. Use after fetch shows a message has attachments (marked with +Natt). Returns file paths ready to Read.

message_id.description: ID of the message with attachments

name: fetch
title: Fetch
description: Fetch recent messages from a channel. Returns oldest-first with message IDs. The platform's search API isn't exposed to bots, so this is the only way to look back.

channel.description: Channel name label (e.g. "main", "general") as configured in channelsConfig

name: schedule_status
title: Schedule Status
description: Show all configured schedules, their next fire time, and whether they are currently running.

name: trigger_schedule
title: Trigger Schedule
description: Manually trigger a named schedule immediately, ignoring time/day constraints.

name: schedule_control
title: Schedule Control
description: Defer or skip a schedule. Use "defer" to suppress for N minutes (default 30), or "skip_today" to suppress for the rest of the day.

minutes.description: Defer duration in minutes (default 30, only for defer action)

name: activate_channel_bridge
title: Activate Channel Bridge
description: Activate or deactivate the channel bridge. When active, inbound messages trigger typing indicators, emoji reactions, and auto-forwarding of transcript output to Discord. When inactive, only direct MCP tool calls (reply, fetch) work.

active.description: true to activate, false to deactivate

name: reload_config
title: Reload Config
description: Reload config from disk and re-register all schedules, webhooks, and event rules without restarting.

name: memory
title: Memory Cycle
description: Run memory operations: cycle2/sleep (promote+dedup), flush, rebuild, prune, cycle1, backfill, status, remember (store fact).

maxDays.description: Age threshold in days for the `prune` action. Unclassified entries older than this are deleted. Default 30, minimum 1. Ignored by other actions.

name: recall
title: Recall
description: Past context from the memory store. `query` accepts a string or an array (parallel fan-out). Async by default: returns an `async_...` handle; merged answer is pushed back via the channel bridge. Pass `wait:true` to block inline. Not for external web (use `search`) or codebase files (use `explore`).

query.description: Natural language query, or an array of queries for multi-angle parallel fan-out.

cwd.description: Optional workspace hint forwarded to the recall agent. Absolute path recommended; `~` expansion supported; forward slashes work on Windows/WSL.

wait.description: Defaults to false (async). Pass `wait:true` to block inline until the merged answer comes back; otherwise the merged answer is pushed into your session automatically via the channel bridge.

name: explore
title: Explore
description: Internal codebase file and code search. `query` accepts a string or array (parallel fan-out). `cwd` is the authoritative search root (no silent fan-out between roots). Async by default: returns `async_...` handle; merged answer is pushed back via the channel bridge, or use `wait:true` inline. Not for past context (use `recall`) or external web (use `search`).

query.description: Natural language query, or an array of queries for multi-angle parallel fan-out.

cwd.description: Authoritative search root. Absolute path recommended; `~` expansion supported; forward slashes work on Windows/WSL. When omitted, the launch workspace is used — pass `cwd: "~/.claude/..."` explicitly to target the plugin install tree or other directories outside the workspace. No silent fan-out between roots.

wait.description: Defaults to false (async). Pass `wait:true` to block inline until the merged answer comes back; otherwise the merged answer is pushed into your session automatically via the channel bridge.

name: search
title: Search
description: External web search, URL scrape, GitHub code/issues/repos. `query` accepts a string or array (parallel fan-out); include a URL to trigger scrape or `owner/repo` for GitHub. Async by default: returns `async_...` handle; merged answer is pushed back via the channel bridge, or use `wait:true` inline. Not for past context (use `recall`) or codebase files (use `explore`).

query.description: Natural language query, or an array of queries for multi-angle parallel fan-out. Include a URL to trigger scrape; mention `owner/repo` for GitHub code/issues.

cwd.description: Optional workspace hint forwarded to the search agent. Rarely needed — only when the query is ambiguous about which repo context applies.

wait.description: Defaults to false (async). Pass `wait:true` to block inline until the merged answer comes back; otherwise the merged answer is pushed into your session automatically via the channel bridge.

name: create_session
title: Create Session
description: Create external AI session with tool access. Auto-injects context. Use preset: full/readonly/mcp. Use agent: Worker/Reviewer.

provider.description: openai, anthropic, gemini, groq, openrouter, xai, copilot, ollama, lmstudio, local

model.description: Provider-specific model id (e.g. "gpt-5", "claude-opus-4", "gemini-2.5-pro"). Must be valid for the chosen provider.

systemPrompt.description: Optional system prompt prepended to the session. When omitted, the plugin injects the default Pool B/C prefix based on role/preset.

preset.description: Tool permission preset: `full` grants read+write+shell, `readonly` restricts to read-only tools, `mcp` exposes only MCP-bridged tools.

files.description: Optional virtual files seeded into the session's working context (path+content pairs). The agent can read these immediately without fs access.

name: list_sessions
title: List Sessions
description: List active orchestrator sessions.

name: close_session
title: Close Session
description: Close an orchestrator session.

sessionId.description: Id of the session to close (as returned by `create_session` or `list_sessions`). Plants a tombstone and aborts any in-flight work; re-closing the same id is a no-op.

name: list_models
title: List Models
description: List available models from all providers.

name: bridge
title: Bridge to External Model
description: Delegate one turn of work to an external agent by role. Role maps to a preset via user-workflow.json (e.g. worker→OPUS XHIGH, reviewer→GPT5.4). Use this to hand off code, research, debug, review, or test work — the lead does not do these directly.

prompt.description: The task instruction for the agent.

role.description: Agent role as defined in user-workflow.json. Default roles: worker, researcher, reviewer, debugger, tester. Users may customize — check user-workflow.json for the actual set.

preset.description: Advanced: explicit preset name (bypass role mapping).

context.description: Extra context appended to the prompt.

file.description: Read prompt from a file path.

name: read
title: Read
description: Read a file from disk with cat -n line-numbered output, byte-size cap, and optional offset/limit windowing.

name: edit
title: Edit
description: Replace one unique occurrence of `old_string` with `new_string` in a file. `old_string` must match exactly once.

name: write
title: Write
description: Create or overwrite a file with the provided content.

path.description: Absolute or cwd-relative file path. File is created if missing, overwritten if it exists.

name: bash
title: Bash
description: Execute a shell command with a configurable timeout. A small blocked-pattern safety list rejects `rm -rf /`, `git push --force`, `format c:`, etc.

command.description: Shell command to execute in the workspace cwd. Rejected if it matches a blocked safety pattern (e.g. `rm -rf /`, `git push --force`, `format c:`).

name: grep
title: Grep
description: ripgrep-backed content search. `pattern` and `glob` both accept a string or an array of strings (OR-joined in one invocation). Output modes: `files_with_matches` (default), `content`, `count`; `head_limit` caps the return set.

pattern.description: Regex pattern(s) passed to ripgrep via `-e`. A string searches for one pattern; an array OR-joins multiple patterns in a single rg invocation.

path.description: Directory or file to search under. Defaults to the workspace cwd (`.`).

glob.description: Optional glob filter(s) applied on top of the default ignore list (node_modules, .git, dist, build, etc.). String or array of strings.

output_mode.description: `files_with_matches` (default, paths only), `content` (matched lines with path and line number), or `count` (per-file match counts).

head_limit.description: Cap on returned entries. Default 100; pass 0 for no limit. Extra entries are summarised as `... [N more entries]`.

offset.description: Skip the first N entries from the ripgrep result before applying `head_limit`. Default 0. Paginate large result sets by combining with `head_limit`.

name: glob
title: Glob
description: File name / path search via ripgrep --files. `pattern` accepts a string or an array of globs (OR-joined in one invocation).

pattern.description: Glob pattern(s) passed to `rg --files --glob`. A string matches one glob; an array OR-joins multiple globs in a single rg invocation.

path.description: Base directory to walk. Defaults to the workspace cwd (`.`). Results are capped at 100 files.

name: multi_edit
title: Multi Edit
description: Apply several ordered replacements to ONE file in a single call. Edits are chained in memory first; if any `old_string` fails to match uniquely (or at all) the file is left untouched. `replace_all:true` per entry drops the uniqueness requirement.

path.description: Absolute or cwd-relative path to the single file receiving all edits.

edits.description: Ordered list of replacements applied in sequence to the in-memory content. Any failure aborts before the file is written, so the tree never lands in a half-edited state.

old_string.description: Exact text to find. Must be unique in the current (post-prior-edits) content unless `replace_all:true`.

replace_all.description: When true, replace every occurrence and skip the uniqueness check. Defaults to false.

name: multi_read
title: Multi Read
description: Read several files in one call. Each entry in `reads` is { path, offset?, limit? } with the same semantics as a single read. Returns `### <path>` delimited sections; per-file errors appear inline and do not abort the batch.

reads.description: List of read requests dispatched in parallel. Each entry reuses the single-read size cap and line-number formatting; per-file errors are inlined into the aggregate instead of aborting the batch.

path.description: Absolute file path (relative paths resolve against the plugin server cwd).

name: batch_edit
title: Batch Edit
description: Apply edits across several files in one call. Each entry in `edits` is { path, old_string, new_string } with the same unique-match rule as a single edit. Per-entry failures are reported inline (`FAIL <path>: <reason>`) and do not abort the batch.

edits.description: Cross-file edits dispatched sequentially through the single-edit path. Each entry is independent; failures are reported inline as `FAIL <path>: <reason>` and the batch continues.

old_string.description: Exact text to find (must be unique in the file).

name: lsp_definition
title: LSP Definition
description: Resolve a symbol to its canonical definition via the TypeScript language server. Pass `symbol` (the name to resolve) and `file` (a TS/JS/MJS file that contains or imports the symbol). Returns one or more `path:line:col` locations. Prefer this over `grep` for any symbol that appears in multiple files — the LSP picks THE definition, grep picks every textual match. Only TypeScript / JavaScript files are supported (.ts .tsx .js .jsx .mjs .cjs).

symbol.description: Symbol name to resolve (identifier, class, function, method).

file.description: Absolute or cwd-relative path to a file that contains or imports the symbol. The server anchors its semantic analysis from this document.

name: lsp_references
title: LSP References
description: List every call site / reference of a symbol across the workspace, using the TypeScript language server. Pass `symbol` and `file` (same contract as `lsp_definition`). Returns a newline-separated `path:line:col` listing, declaration included. Unlike grep, unrelated occurrences of the same name (different scopes, shadowed vars) are excluded.

symbol.description: Symbol name to look up references for.

file.description: Absolute or cwd-relative path to a file that contains the symbol. Used to anchor the LSP cursor.

name: lsp_symbols
title: LSP Document Symbols
description: Outline a TS/JS file: class / function / method / variable declarations with line numbers, hierarchical when the server supports it. Pass `file`. Good first pass before diving in — shows the shape of a file without reading its body.

file.description: Absolute or cwd-relative path to the TS/JS file to outline.
